summary.name: Madanaphala
summary.scientific_name: Catunaregam spinosa (Thunb.) Tirveng. (Syn. Randia dumetorum Lam.)

plant_profile.history: Unique History: Classified under the 'Vamanopaga' (emetic-assisting) and 'Deepaniya' (appetizer) groups in ancient Ayurvedic treatises like Charaka Samhita. The name 'Madanaphala' literally translates to 'fruit of Madana' (referring to neurological or intoxicating effects). It is spiritually associated with 'Sattva' (purity and clarity). Mentioned in classical Sanskrit literature as the 'Mountain Pomegranate'. Physical Characteristics: A small-to-medium-sized deciduous tree reaching up to 5-10 meters in height, with a majestic crown and brownish-grey, rough bark. The leaves are opposite, ovate-elliptic, and leathery. The flowers are attractive, white-to-yellowish, fragrant, in terminal or axillary cymes. The fruit is a globose berry (orange when ripe) containing numerous flat seeds. It is found throughout India, particularly in the sub-Himalayan tract and deciduous forests. Specific Chemical Compounds: Primarily contains Triterpene Saponins (Randianin), Flavonoids, and Saponins. The presence of saponins is crucial for its neuro-relaxant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-tubercular effects (when Appropriately diluted). Strict Standardizations: The fruit is standardized in API based on its yellowish-brown-to-black color and characteristic estremamente amaro-pungente taste. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<6%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>5%), and water-soluble extractive (>15%). HPLC is used to monitor 1% to 2% saponins in commercial extracts.

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_modulation: Powerful Tridoshahara (pacifies all three doshas). Due to its Tikta (bitter) and Kashaya (astringent) Rasa and Ushna (heating) Virya, it is the most prestigious herb for 'Vamana' (therapeutic emesis) and 'Deepana-Pachana' (digestive) therapies, clearing the cold, heavy, and sticky qualities of Kapha through its systemic warming and upward-moving (Urdhvabhaga) property. It is arguably the most supreme emetic herb.

medicinal_uses.expert_vamana_and_emetic_specialist: Madanaphala is the premier Ayurvedic remedy for all conditions requiring therapeutic emesis (Vamana). Its 'Vamanopaga' (emetic-assisting) and 'Tikshna' (sharp) nature provided by triterpene saponins helps in expelling deep-seated Kapha and metabolic toxins (Ama) from the upper gastrointestinal tract. WHO-aligned research supports its role in managing inflammatory lung load.
medicinal_uses.respiratory_and_asthma_management: Historically known as 'Shwasahara' (antiasthmatic). It effectively reduces the intensity and frequency of asthma attacks by clearing 'Kapha-avarana' (mucus-obstruction) in the respiratory channels and providing systemic warming (Ushna).
medicinal_uses.liver_and_spleen_stimulant: Acts as a powerful agent for managing liver health. It is traditionally used to treat jaundice and fatty liver by promoting healthy bile secretion and clearing congestion in the 'Yakṛt' (liver) system through its systemic upward-moving energy.
medicinal_uses.skin_health_and_wound_healing: Its antimicrobial and blood-purifying properties help in treating chronic skin conditions and localized inflammation. The paste applied topically promotes rapid relief from acne and sun-burns.
medicinal_uses.digestive_and_metabolism_specialist: Its Tikta (bitter) and Ushna (heating) nature helps in igniting the Jatharagni (digestive fire) and cleared stagnant food from the gut. It is traditionally used to manage 'Anaha' (bloating) and severe indigestion.
medicinal_uses.worm_and_internal_parasite_care: Its extremely bitter nature makes it incomparable for managing stubborn intestinal parasites and worms (Krimi) that thrive in a high-Kapha environment. It helps in cleared stagnant toxins from the gut.
medicinal_uses.phytochemical_profile: Rich in Triterpene Saponins (Randianin, Dumetoronin), Flavonoids, Steroids, and diversos Amino Acids. Randianin is the primary bioactive responsible for its potent emetic and anti-inflammatory action.

preparation_methods.fruit_powder__churna_: 1 gram to 3 grams of fine powder taken twice daily with honey or warm milk for enhancing memory and vitality.
preparation_methods.fruit_decoction__kwatha_: 5g of dried fruit boiled in 100ml water reduced to 25ml for therapeutic emesis (Vamana) under expert care.
preparation_methods.madanaphala_pippali: 500mg tablettaken after meals for managing chronic asthma and respiratory congestion through its 'Tikshna' quality.
preparation_methods.medicated_oil__taila_: Fruit or root processed in sesame oil; used for local scalp massage (hair growth) and joint pain (Shothahara).
preparation_methods.external_paste: Ground fresh fruit or paste applied directly to localized inflammations (Mumps, boils, skin rashes) to reduce heat and swelling.

dosage.adults: Powder: 1-3 grams; Decoction: 20-50 ml; Vamana: 10-30 g (expert only), divided throughout the day before meals.
dosage.children: Generally safe (in very small doses). 25 mg to 125 mg of powder mixed with honey once or twice daily for digestive protection during childhood infections.
dosage.elderly: Ideal for age-related Vata issues and maintaining cardiac health. Dosage: 1g of powder daily with warm milk at bedtime.

contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with high Pitta (burning sensation, hyperacidity) should use it in moderation as it is Ushna (heating). Toxicity is low, but emetic effects can be severe if taken in excess.
side_effects: None documented at traditional therapeutic levels. Excessive use in very sensitive individuals may rarely cause mild skin cooling or stomach dryness. Discontinue if extreme emesis causes nausea.
drug_interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anti-depressant or cardiac medications as it may potentiate their effects. No severe adverse interactions reported in clinical literature.
safety.toxicity_notes: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb and fruit with a history of use spanning millennia across various global traditional systems (Ayurveda, Unani, and Himachali medicine) without documented toxicity.
